title: Pithecellobium aruense
intro: Pithecellobium aruense, also known as the 'Aru Tree', is a species of flowering plant in the family Fabaceae, native to tropical regions of the Pacific islands. The plant has been traditionally used in folk medicine, with reported health benefits including the treatment of fever, rheumatism, and digestive issues. The bioactive constituents of Pithecellobium aruense include flavonoids, alkaloids, and saponins, which have been found to exhibit antioxidant, anti-inflammatory, and antimicrobial properties. The most common herbal preparations made with this plant include decoctions and infusions, often used to treat various health ailments, as well as a medicinal wine made by infusing the plant's leaves in a spirit.
taxonomy: Pithecellobium aruense is classified within the family Fabaceae, which is commonly known as the legume or pea family. This classification places it among other flowering plants that produce fruits in the form of pods. Further, it belongs to the subfamily Faboideae, which comprises many species of legumes. Pithecellobium aruense is a part of the genus Pithecellobium, a group of tropical and subtropical tree species. The species is native to South America.
